techniques.evidence_quote: account DevOps1-bpvbp had been used to create an unknown account DevOps3 on 26 Oct 2022 at 21:29:03 UTC

techniques.technique_name: Command and Scripting Interpreter: Unix Shell
techniques.evidence_quote: ssm-user switched user to the default Amazon Linux2 account ec2-user which initiated a new bash session
DevOps1-bpvbp account had initiated a StartSession which provided a connection to EC2 instance i-08e56d6b6c0439daf using Session Manager
A new super user account was found and no one recognised it which triggered an incident... the first event we find related to the user is the CreateUser event

techniques.technique_name: Abuse Elevation Control Mechanism: Sudo and Sudo Caching
techniques.evidence_quote: there is log evidence that indicates that sudo command was executed to switch user to ec2-user during the attacker's session
The first command in the code box above, shows the Instance MetaData Service (IMDS) being used to extract information regarding the Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) Id.

the Instance MetaData Service (IMDS) being used to extract information regarding the Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) Id. The VPC Id is then used in conjunction with the aws command line tool to provide detailed information of the security group that the instance belongs to, in particular looking for port 22 entries

in particular looking for port 22 entries, port 22 is commonly associated with SSH. This could be seen as a method to perform network discovery to find other hosts that can be used for lateral movement.
the ec2-user known-hosts file was accessed... the ssh commands are linked to the attacker's session... connected to another host i-020e722274233c7f2 for additional suspicious activity
the attacker was very much interested in information from the staff table in the postgres database... after the last command was executed /tmp/staff.txt was created

copied the database dump file staff.txt file from i-020e722274233c7f2 to i-08e56d6b6c0439daf... created a new s3 bucket and copied the database dump file staff.txt to it. This could be seen as staging data for exfiltration.
show the aws command line to create a S3 bucket and the file staff.txt was copied to it. This could be seen as staging data for exfiltration.

attack_chain.description: Attacker used compromised IAM account DevOps1-bpvbp (no MFA enabled) to authenticate to the AWS Management Console from IP 185.195.232.111
attack_chain.evidence_quote: We can see that this user did not have MFA enabled. We can also see the source IP address from where the user logged in on the console.

attack_chain.description: Attacker used DevOps1-bpvbp to create a new super user IAM account named DevOps3 via the AWS Management Console at 21:29:03 UTC on 26-10-2022, likely for use as a persistence backdoor
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the first event we find related to the user is the CreateUser event... A new super user account was found and no one recognised it... one of the options is to use it as a persistence method.

attack_chain.description: Attacker initiated an AWS SSM Session Manager session from DevOps1-bpvbp to EC2 instance i-08e56d6b6c0439daf at 21:29:53 UTC, gaining interactive shell access
attack_chain.evidence_quote: a StartSession event was recorded with the same account... a session was started from the account DevOps1-bpvbp towards an instance with identifier i-08e56d6b6c0439daf

attack_chain.technique_name: Abuse Elevation Control Mechanism: Sudo and Sudo Caching
attack_chain.description: On EC2 instance i-08e56d6b6c0439daf, the attacker (operating as ssm-user) used sudo to switch to the ec2-user account, initiating a new bash session under process ID 32627
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the ssm-user switched user to the default Amazon Linux2 account ec2-user which initiated a new bash session... log evidence that indicates that sudo command was executed to switch user to ec2-user during the attacker's session

attack_chain.technique_name: Unsecured Credentials: Cloud Instance Metadata API
attack_chain.description: Attacker queried the EC2 Instance Metadata Service (IMDS) from i-08e56d6b6c0439daf to retrieve VPC ID information for subsequent network reconnaissance
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the Instance MetaData Service (IMDS) being used to extract information regarding the Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) Id.

attack_chain.description: Attacker used the AWS CLI with the retrieved VPC ID to enumerate security group details on the EC2 instance, specifically searching for port 22 (SSH) entries to identify lateral movement targets
attack_chain.evidence_quote: The VPC Id is then used in conjunction with the aws command line tool to provide detailed information of the security group that the instance belongs to, in particular looking for port 22 entries

attack_chain.description: Attacker used SSH from i-08e56d6b6c0439daf to laterally move to EC2 instance i-020e722274233c7f2 (10.0.8.25), with known_hosts file access confirming SSH activity
attack_chain.evidence_quote: The ec2-user known-hosts file was accessed and... the ssh commands are linked to the attacker's session... connected to another host i-020e722274233c7f2 for additional suspicious activity

attack_chain.description: On the second EC2 instance i-020e722274233c7f2, the attacker queried the PostgreSQL database's staff table and dumped the results to /tmp/staff.txt
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the attacker was very much interested in information from the staff table in the postgres database... after the last command was executed /tmp/staff.txt was created

attack_chain.description: Attacker used SCP to copy staff.txt from i-020e722274233c7f2 back to i-08e56d6b6c0439daf, consolidating the stolen data on the initial beachhead instance
attack_chain.evidence_quote: copied the database dump file staff.txt file from i-020e722274233c7f2 to i-08e56d6b6c0439daf... in our timeline we can see the file staff.txt was created during the attacker's session which is likely to be the result of the scp command.

attack_chain.description: Attacker created a new S3 bucket using the AWS CLI and copied staff.txt into it, staging the stolen database dump for exfiltration
attack_chain.evidence_quote: show the aws command line to create a S3 bucket and the file staff.txt was copied to it. This could be seen as staging data for exfiltration.

initial_access_vector: Compromised IAM account DevOps1-bpvbp (no MFA) used to log into the AWS Management Console from external IP 185.195.232.111
persistence_mechanism: New super-user IAM account "DevOps3" created via IAM CreateUser API call through the AWS Management Console using the compromised DevOps1-bpvbp account
privilege_escalation: On EC2 instance i-08e56d6b6c0439daf, attacker escalated from ssm-user to ec2-user via sudo command within the SSM Session Manager interactive session
